title: gonolobus suberosus var. granulatus
intro: Gonolobus suberosus var. granulatus, also known as the thorny coral milkflower, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to Central America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its use as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which is attributed to the presence of various flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Gonolobus suberosus var. granulatus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
